div.p: Same PID — Completely Different Process Image — All Cached Pointers Invalid

g.text: ASLR: Why Addresses Change on Every exec()
1. Flush all VMAs → munmap entire address space
3. Allocate fresh stack at NEW random base (ASLR)
4. Map new heap starting at NEW brk address
5. Load new ld-linux, libc at NEW randomized addrs
6. Reset CR3 → new page table root (same PID, new PGD)

text.tspan: /* PID is the same — but address space is brand new */
/* PID is the same — but address space is brand new */

g.text: Stack base | mmap_rnd_bits=28 | 256MB random offset | rsp varies
Heap base | brk randomized | 16MB random offset | brk varies
libs base | mmap_rnd_bits=28 | 256MB random offset | libc varies
PIE text | exec_rnd_bits=8 | 16MB random offset | main() varies
CONSEQUENCE: cached pointer 0x7FFD3400 maps to DIFFERENT physical page
Stop 1 | SIGTRAP|0x80 | execve entry stop | in_syscall→1 | read args: orig_rax=59
Stop 2 | SIGTRAP event=0x04 | PTRACE_EVENT_EXEC | NOT a syscall stop — call handle_exec_event()
Stop 3 | SIGTRAP|0x80 | execve EXIT stop | in_syscall→0 | rax=0 (success)
Stop 4 | SIGTRAP|0x80 | python3 first entry| in_syscall→1 | new program running
DANGER: if PTRACE_EVENT_EXEC mistakenly treated as syscall stop → toggle corruption

text.tspan: /* BAD: tracer reads cached ptr AFTER exec */
/* Result: EFAULT — page unmapped in python3 */
/* OR: reads unrelated python3 stack data */
/* Either way: WRONG data, silent corruption */
/* CORRECT: reset cached_str_ptr on EXEC event */
/* Never read stale pointers after exec */
/* BAD: tracer reads cached ptr AFTER exec */
/* Result: EFAULT — page unmapped in python3 */
/* OR: reads unrelated python3 stack data */
/* Either way: WRONG data, silent corruption */
/* CORRECT: reset cached_str_ptr on EXEC event */
/* Never read stale pointers after exec */
